headline: Abandoned Projects Leave 18,000 Home Buyers Stranded
context: KPKT recorded 85 abandoned housing projects involving 18,000 buyer units with total losses estimated at RM4.8 billion.

cards.big: 85 abandoned projects strand 18,000 buyers with RM4.8 billion losses
cards.sub: Many buyers continue paying mortgages on homes that will never be completed

cards.big: 62% of abandoned projects in Selangor Johor and Perak by developer count
cards.sub: Common factors include developer insolvency overruns and regulatory failures

cards.big: 12,000 buyers continue servicing bank loans averaging RM1,800 monthly
cards.sub: Banks refuse restructuring citing existing property as collateral despite non-completion

cards.big: Build-then-sell mandated since 2023 applies only to new projects not existing
cards.sub: Buyers of pre-2023 projects have no protection under the new framework

cards.big: Abandoned projects test whether buyer protection is retroactive or only prospective
18,000 buyers stranded by abandoned projects. Developer insurance should be mandatory before construction begins.
